title: Check if a QR code on a letter you've received from HMRC is genuine
body: "Letters and leaflets from HMRC may contain a QR code which you can scan for further information or help.  If your letter is not listed on this page:   check if a letter you’ve received from HMRC is genuine  check HMRC contact that uses more than one communication method     Report suspicious phone calls, emails or texts to HMRC.   Child Benefit leaflet — claim online  HMRC provides hospitals with leaflets for new parents which encourages them to claim Child Benefit online after registering their baby’s birth.  The QR code on this leaflet takes you to our guidance on how to make a Child Benefit claim.  HMRC app leaflet and envelope  In some letters you receive, you may find:   an HMRC app leaflet a QR code on the envelope   The QR code on the envelope and the leaflet takes you to our guidance about the HMRC app, which includes information about:   what the app does how to access the app   Letter CGT LPP — pay taxes, penalties or enquiry settlements  You may receive a letter if you have been charged a late payment penalty.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on paying taxes, penalties or enquiry settlements.  Letter CH(A)410 — termination notification  You may receive a letter if you are no longer entitled to Child Benefit.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on asking us to reconsider a Child Benefit or Guardian’s Allowance decision.  Letter CH(A)1700 — award notification  You may receive a letter regarding your entitlement if you applied for or receive Child Benefit.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on Child Benefit payment dates.  Letter CH(A)1708 — High Income Child Benefit Charge opt in or out notification  You may receive a letter if you have opted in or out of getting Child Benefit payments.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on High Income Child Benefit Charge.  Letter CHB297B — full time non-advanced education  You will receive a letter before your child turns 16, if you claim Child Benefit. This letter will:   ask you to confirm if your child is staying in education or training include a QR code that takes you to our guidance where you can extend your claim online, if you need to   Letter CTY50 — charities  You may receive a letter with the outcome of your application to act as an official for a charitable organisation.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on who can run your charity’s finances.  Letter EA2 — Employment Allowance  You may receive a letter regarding your entitlement if you applied or receive Employment Allowance.  The QR code on the letter takes you to our guidance on Employment Allowance. You can find out:   if you are eligible how to claim   Letter MGD7 — Machine Games Duty  You may receive a notice of assessment when our records show a Machine Games Duty return is outstanding.  The QR code on the letter takes you...
